_comment: Tirzepatide percent body-weight change dose-response in obesity, 2 SURMOUNT Phase 3 RCTs. Per-arm LSM change-from-baseline + SE extracted from AACT snapshot 2026-04-12 (outcome_measurements.txt, primary outcome only). SE converted to SD via sd = se * sqrt(n) for engine use. Both trials are placebo-controlled and use the SAME molecule (tirzepatide), SAME route (SC once weekly), SAME primary endpoint (% body weight change from baseline at week 72), and SAME background-therapy core (lifestyle/diet/exercise intervention). SURMOUNT-1 enrolled adults WITHOUT T2D (4 arms: placebo / 5 / 10 / 15 mg). SURMOUNT-2 enrolled adults WITH T2D (3 arms: placebo / 10 / 15 mg — the 5 mg arm was deliberately omitted per regulatory choice for the T2D-obesity indication). The trial set is intentionally small (k=2) — this is the real-world reality of the obesity dose-response literature at the time SURMOUNT-3/-4 were not yet posting AACT primary results. Engine HKSJ floor max(1, Q/(k-1)) is at its small-k boundary here. Per-arm analyzed N from outcome_counts.txt (mITT analysis population for primary efficacy estimand). Cross-checked vs published primary publications via PubMed MCP — both trials use the efficacy estimand (on-treatment, excluding data after prematurely stopping study drug) for the AACT-registered primary outcome; SURMOUNT-1 PubMed abstract reports treatment-regimen estimand (-15.0/-19.5/-20.9/-3.1) which DIFFERS from the AACT efficacy estimand (-16.0/-21.4/-22.5/-2.4), and SURMOUNT-2 PubMed abstract similarly reports treatment-regimen estimand (-12.8/-14.7/-3.2) DIFFERING from AACT efficacy (-13.4/-15.7/-3.3). Both estimands are pre-registered FDA-acceptable; AACT efficacy estimand used here for cross-trial uniformity within this fixture (same source-of-truth choice as SURPASS fixture; both fixtures use AACT-primary as the canonical source).
source: Tirzepatide percent body weight change dose-response, 2 SURMOUNT Phase 3 trials (AACT 2026-04-12)
dose_assignment_method: as-randomized per-trial dose (5/10/15 mg subcutaneous once weekly; SURMOUNT-2 omits 5 mg)
class_equivalence_caveat: Both trials use the SAME molecule (tirzepatide) and SAME route (subcutaneous once-weekly), so the pooled slope is a within-drug dose-response curve. Trials differ in population (obesity without T2D vs obesity WITH T2D), and the T2D trial deliberately omits the 5 mg arm — both design differences contribute to between-trial heterogeneity and the dose-response curve in SURMOUNT-2 has no representation at the 5 mg level. k=2 stresses the HKSJ floor max(1, Q/(k-1)) at its small-k boundary.
active_comparator_handling: Not applicable: both trials are placebo-controlled. Placebo is the dose=0 reference (is_reference=true) in both trials.
se_to_sd_conversion: AACT reports LEAST_SQUARES_MEAN with Standard Error dispersion. Converted to SD via sd = se * sqrt(n) using the per-arm analyzed N from outcome_counts.txt. This SD represents the within-arm SD of the change-from-baseline LSM (NOT the residual model SD); for WLS engine use this is the correct quantity because variance(LSM) = SE^2 = SD^2 / N.
small_k_caveat: k=2 trials. Engine HKSJ Q-floor max(1, Q/(k-1)) is at its degenerate small-k boundary: Q/(k-1) = Q (denominator = 1), so any Q < 1 triggers the floor. This fixture is intended as a small-k stress test for the engine's HKSJ implementation. Do not treat the pooled dose-response slope as a definitive obesity-class meta-analytic estimate without additional tirzepatide-obesity trials (SURMOUNT-3 maintenance, SURMOUNT-4 cessation withdrawal, SURMOUNT-MMO outcome trial).
pubmed_retrieval_notes: Both PMIDs verified via PubMed MCP get_article_metadata. DOIs cross-checked. Per the user contract spec, the initially-proposed SURMOUNT-2 PMID 37356446 was VERIFIED WRONG (that PMID is GBD 2021 Diabetes burden paper Lancet 2023;402(10397):203-234); the correct SURMOUNT-2 PMID is 37385275 (Garvey et al. Lancet 2023;402(10402):613-626, doi:10.1016/S0140-6736(23)01200-X), confirmed via search by title+author. According to PubMed: SURMOUNT-1 (PMID 35658024, doi:10.1056/NEJMoa2206038, Jastreboff et al. NEJM 2022;387(3):205-216), SURMOUNT-2 (PMID 37385275, doi:10.1016/S0140-6736(23)01200-X, Garvey et al. Lancet 2023;402(10402):613-626).

trials.background_therapy: lifestyle intervention (reduced-calorie diet + increased physical activity)
trials.population: Adults with BMI >=30, or BMI >=27 with at least one weight-related complication, excluding T2D; baseline mean weight 104.8 kg, mean BMI 38.0
trials.data_provenance: AACT 2026-04-12 outcome_measurements.txt (outcome_id=211171518). All four arms (placebo / 5 / 10 / 15 mg) in same PRIMARY outcome. LSM and SE direct from AACT. N per arm from outcome_counts.txt (mITT). AACT uses the EFFICACY estimand (on-treatment, excluding data after prematurely stopping study drug); PubMed NEJM abstract reports the TREATMENT-REGIMEN estimand (-15.0/-19.5/-20.9/-3.1). Both pre-registered FDA-acceptable. AACT efficacy values used here for source-of-truth uniformity within fixture.

trials.studlab: SURMOUNT-2 (Garvey 2023, obesity with T2D, no 5 mg arm)
trials.background_therapy: lifestyle intervention (reduced-calorie diet + increased physical activity); existing T2D antihyperglycaemic regimen continued (metformin, SGLT2i, sulfonylureas allowed; insulin excluded)
trials.population: Adults with BMI >=27 kg/m^2 AND HbA1c 7.0-10.0% on stable antihyperglycaemic therapy; baseline mean weight 100.7 kg, mean BMI 36.1, mean HbA1c 8.02%; randomized 1:1:1 to tirz 10 / tirz 15 / placebo (NO 5 mg arm)
trials.data_provenance: AACT 2026-04-12 outcome_measurements.txt (outcome_id=211207789). All three arms (placebo / 10 / 15 mg; NO 5 mg) in same PRIMARY outcome. LSM and SE direct from AACT. N per arm from outcome_counts.txt (mITT). AACT uses the EFFICACY estimand (on-treatment, only participants with non-missing baseline AND post-baseline values; excludes treatment-discontinuation observations); PubMed Lancet abstract reports the TREATMENT-REGIMEN estimand (-12.8/-14.7/-3.2). Both pre-registered FDA-acceptable. AACT efficacy values used here for source-of-truth uniformity within fixture.
